scenario: A local macOS app shell lets a user search Codex/OpenClaw/future Claude Code sessions through the same public-safe recall surfaces, inspect safe summaries and source refs, filter by project/status/priority, and hand high-signal refs to an agent without raw transcript rendering.
expected_public_safe_evidence: query string with no private transcript text
live tool names when connected through CLI, MCP, or OpenClaw gateway
local_mac_shell_ready runtime marker when claiming connected local Mac UI proof
project, status, and priority filter state
selected bounded expansion profile and token budget
structured loo_desktop_act blockers for missing or mismatched live desktop proof fields
desktop live/no-focus proof harness blockers or readiness status
desktop proof-action catalog/callable status, fail-closed blocker for generic invocation, or public-safe TextEdit scratch launch observation with exact approved args
desktop GUI proof-report blocker, approval-fixture status, and runtime marker path when valid
Codex Desktop coherence state showing whether a target thread is cli_visible, desktop_visible, desktop_refresh_required, desktop_restart_required, or unknown
blocker codes for unavailable local DB or plugin tools
pass_criteria: User can search indexed sessions and see safe summaries with source refs.
User can filter results by project, status, priority, and blocker state.
User can copy source refs and selected public-safe summary text for an OpenClaw agent.
User can request bounded expansion through existing loo_* profiles before more detail is shown.
The live CLI proof mode records read-only loo_* tool source metadata without raw transcript rendering.
The connected runtime proof only clears local_mac_shell_ready when the shell is actually ready on macOS.
The shell must fail closed when local DB access, required plugin tools, or OpenClaw plugin loading is unavailable.
CUA and Peekaboo appear as readiness/status surfaces only unless a future backend-specific approval gate is proven.
Live loo_desktop_act requests stay blocked and return a resumable proof checklist for OpenClaw agents.
Desktop live/no-focus proof harness output prepares an action-bound proof plan without performing GUI mutation.

Desktop proof-action output is limited to the CUA Driver TextEdit scratch launch path, fails closed when generic gateway invocation lacks exact proof args, and excludes raw stdout, screenshots, and scratch file paths from public evidence.
Desktop GUI proof-report output validates supplied public-safe observations and emits release-compatible approval/runtime marker artifacts without itself performing GUI mutation.

Codex Desktop coherence classification does not perform a Desktop action and must not claim Desktop-visible collaboration when the state is cli_visible, desktop_refresh_required, desktop_restart_required, or unknown.
fail_criteria: UI renders raw Codex transcripts or raw prompts.
UI captures screenshots or videos as public evidence.
UI performs live GUI mutation or live Codex control without explicit approval.
UI claims one-click install, Claude Code adapter parity, unattended takeover, or release-ready macOS app status.
UI falls back to raw file reads when plugin tools or the local DB are unavailable.
known_gaps: The repo includes a static prototype shell and live CLI proof mode, but no packaged macOS app shell has been produced.
No signed or notarized app artifact has been produced.
No live OpenClaw gateway UI event-loop smoke has been run.
A Peekaboo scratch-window no-focus proof exists for one approved TextEdit window move; this does not prove generic GUI mutation or Codex GUI mutation.

A CUA Driver scratch-window no-focus proof-action path exists for one approved TextEdit launch_app action; generic OpenClaw gateway invocation without exact proof args fails closed and this does not prove generic GUI mutation, Codex GUI mutation, prompt typing, clicking, or arbitrary app control.
Codex Desktop coherence now has an explicit classifier, but runtime evidence still needs to prove whether direct-protocol activity appears in Codex Desktop without refresh/restart.
Claude Code remains a future adapter boundary, not a proved equivalent adapter.
next_action: Keep desktop collaboration out of release claims unless a future lane provides packaged app or backend-specific runtime proof for the exact claimed action.
proof_boundary: Scored as an advisory public-safe beta boundary review for existing local Mac shell, Peekaboo scratch-window proof, CUA Driver scratch-window proof for one approved TextEdit launch_app action, desktop proof-action evidence through issue #278, and Codex Desktop coherence classification through issue #307, not generic GUI mutation. It does not prove a signed or release-ready macOS app, one-click install, Claude Code adapter parity, live Codex control, Codex GUI mutation, Desktop-visible live collaboration, prompt typing, clicking, arbitrary app control, or safe desktop automation.
